gpt-4.1-nano.Description: Fastest and cheapest OpenAI GPT model. Ideal for simple extraction tasks like classification.

gpt-4o-mini.Description: Highly affordable and fast OpenAI model optimized for high-volume text and structured data tasks with multimodal capabilities.

gpt-5.4-nano.Description: Smallest OpenAI GPT-5 model. Best for high-volume, simple extraction at low cost.

gpt-5.4-mini.Description: Fast, capable, strong OpenAI GPT model that has multimodal understanding at mid-range cost.

gemini-2.5-flash-lite.Description: The smallest and most cost effective Google AI model, built for at scale usage.

gemini-2.5-flash.Description: A hybrid reasoning Google AI model which supports a 1M token context window and has thinking budgets.

gemini-3-flash-preview.Description: An intelligent Google AI model built for speed, combining frontier intelligence with superior search and grounding.

Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct.Description: The 'Systems Analyst' choice. Specifically trained on code and logic. This model is much less likely to make rounding errors or break JSON syntax compared to general models.

Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct.Description: Alibaba's flagship 72B vision-language model designed for expert-level multimodal analysis. It features elite performance in document parsing, chart-to-JSON extraction, and long-form video understanding (1hr+). With enhanced 'Agentic' capabilities, it can autonomously navigate complex GUIs and mobile interfaces, making it ideal for high-precision retail automation and strategic data mining.

Qwen/Qwen3.5-Flash.Description: 2026's efficiency leader. Uses a native hybrid architecture to provide near-instant response times for text-based calculations and massive 1M token context support.

Qwen/Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking.Description: A 'Reasoning' model that shows its work. It will double-check its math logic internally before outputting the final JSON, making it ideal for invoices with complex tax rules.

meta-llama/Llama-4-Scout-17B.Description: The efficiency king of the Llama 4 family. A 17B Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model that punches like a 70B. Incredible at following strict JSON schemas for invoice processing.

meta-llama/Llama-4-Maverick-109B.Description: A heavy-duty reasoning model with a 1M token context window. Ideal for batch-processing hundreds of invoice JSONs in a single pass with high mathematical precision.

meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct.Description: A battle-tested legacy model from late 2024/2025. Extremely reliable for basic text calculations and stable JSON formatting.

meta-llama/Llama-4-8B-Nano.Description: The cheapest Llama 4 option. Best for simple line-item summing and basic data cleanup where latency must be near-zero.

mistral-nemo-12b.Description: An open-weight model with the lowest blended cost. Excellent for basic data formatting and summaries.

microsoft/phi-4.Description: A 14B parameter model specializing in complex reasoning and math. Ideal for precise invoice calculations and structured JSON data processing.

qwen/Qwen3-Coder-32B.Description: Specialized for code and logic. The best model on Groq for ensuring your JSON structure remains perfectly valid while handling complex tax and discount calculations.

meta-llama/Llama-4-8B-Nano.Description: The fastest model available. Best for simple line-item summing where speed is the primary requirement. Processes virtually instantly.

meta-llama/Llama-4-Scout-17B.Description: The efficiency king on Groq's LPU. A 17B model that processes at 500+ tokens/sec. Exceptional at following strict JSON schemas and validating arithmetic for high-volume invoice batches.

mistral-ai/Mixtral-8x7B-32k.Description: The 'Value' workhorse. Uses a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture to provide reliable reasoning at a flat, low rate. Excellent for long-form JSON extraction.
